name: Aniruddha
dates_notes: Aniruddha is commonly placed around the fifteenth century as an early commentator on the Sāṅkhya-Sūtra before Vijñānabhikṣu's later Sāṅkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya.
core_thesis: Aniruddha is known for the Sāṅkhya-Sūtra-Vṛtti, an early commentary on the Sāṅkhya-Sūtra. The work belongs to the later sūtra-based Sāṅkhya tradition rather than to the older Sāṅkhya-Kārikā line alone. Its importance is chronological and exegetical: it witnesses how Sāṅkhya categories such as prakṛti, puruṣa, the guṇas, satkāryavāda, bondage, and kaivalya were read in a sūtra-commentarial format before Vijñānabhikṣu's Vedānta-Yoga-Sāṅkhya synthesis. Because the clean text was not located in this run, this entry avoids specific doctrinal claims beyond the standard Sāṅkhya commitments attached to the work.

engaged_works.title: Sankhya-Sutra-Vrtti
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Traditionally and scholarly attributed to Aniruddha as a commentary on the Sāṅkhya-Sūtra.
engaged_works.summary: A commentary on the Sāṅkhya-Sūtra. The Vṛtti explains the sūtra presentation of Sāṅkhya's core categories and arguments: the distinction between prakṛti and puruṣa, the plurality of puruṣas, the guṇas, satkāryavāda, the production of the tattvas, bondage through non-discrimination, and liberation through discriminative knowledge. It is important as a pre-Vijñānabhikṣu witness to sūtra-based Sāṅkhya exegesis.
